ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Thomas Lucas. Gilbert Jay in Propria Persona and specially appearing for Counsel Greer Smith for William Gray. Ed Thirkell for and with Russell Moss.

Mr. Jay stated that they mediated, that the matter was resolved and that he was here to support Mr. Thirkell's petition. In addition, Mr. Jay stated that he had not filed his petition for fees and that Ms. Cupido passed away in September. Ms. Rasch stated that she had no objection to the petition or the fees, that she prepared a declaration in response to the Examiner's notes and that she had the original documents. Mr. Jay also stated that he and Ms. Greer still planned to file their petitions for fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Alzira Cupido. Ed Thirkell for Russell Moss.

Counsel stated that settlement had been reached and that they were awaiting signatures from Mr. Devenere and Mr. Tolpen. Mr. Jay confirmed that he had withdrawn his objection filed on April 15, 2009. The Court approved the accounting, and granted the petition (signing an order for the same in open court). Mr. Thirkell was directed to file the petition for approval of the settlement which the Court authorized to be heard on June 9, 2009. Petitions for fees will be filed and addressed at a later date.

Amended Petn. Of Settlor And Beneficiary For Relief And Redress And For Order Bringing Grantor Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Oct-17-2008 Advanced From Dec-16-08 To Oct-21-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orde To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-17-2008 Advanced From Oct-27-08 To Oct-21-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orde To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 291196 (Conservatorship of Alzira Alves Cupido).

Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Alzira Cupido on the trust matter and in Propria Persona/Interested person on the Conservatorship matter. Julia Lingys and Ed Thirkell for Russell Moss. Pauline Sloan (Specially appearing for Nancy Rasch) for Thomas Lucas. Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for the Conservatee. Greer Smith for William Gray.

The Parties informed the Court that they agreed to have Thomas Lucas appointed as the Conservator of the person only, that Ms. Cupido would continue to have full access to her social security benefits, that all power of attorney for health care and finances are revoked and that there was no exclusive medical authority, (order for the same to be signed upon review). Ms. O'Keeffe was discharged as Court Appointed Counsel for the Conservatee. The Trust matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court). Mr. Moss is to continue to provide funds for payment of bills.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

Amended Petn. Of Settlor And Beneficiary For Relief And Redress And For Order Bringing Grantor Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Oct-21-2008 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Bringing Grantor Trust Under Court Supervision; Affirming William R. Gray As Successor Trustee; And Compelling Immediate Surrender Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-10-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gilbert Jay for and with Alzira Cupido (Trustor). Greer Smith for and with William Gray (Successor Trustee). Ed Thirkell and Julia Lingys for and with Russell Moss. Mary Gemma O'Keeffe court appointed Counsel for Alzira Cupido Gagliola in the conservatorship matter also present.

Mr. Jay stated that the Clerk would not issue a citation without a court order and provided case history. The parties raised issues regarding the current status of Mr. Moss, the Trust, the medical reports, quality of care being provided by Kaiser and payment of bills for health care and payment to the Caregiver. After hearing oral argument, the court stated that it saw no urgency, that it would review the medical reports in camera and that a determination would be made as to the trust matter and the conservatorhsip matter being heard at the same time. The Court directed Mr. Jay to turn over the Caregiver's social security number and all incurred bills to Mr. Moss for payment.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Order Bringing Revocable Trust Under Court Supervision And For Appt. Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-15-2008 Off Calendar

Petition Of Settlor And Beneficiary For Relief And Redress
Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Bringing Revocable Trust Under Court Supervision And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-04-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Alzira Alves Cupido. Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch. Greer Smith for and with William Gray. Gilbert Jay in Pro Per.

Mr. Siracusa stated that the purpose of the stipulation was that there was litigation pending against Mr. Moss with the current trust and that Mr. Moss was not willing to step down if Mr. Gray was to be appointed. Mr. Siracusa also stated that Mr. Gray was not a licensed fiduciary, that his client was not opposed to having Ms. Dolch serve and that there was no petition for removal before the court today. Mr. Jay stated that Mr. Gray was elected by Ms. Gagliolo to serve as Succesor Trustee on March 23, 2008 and that she released Mr. Moss at the same time. Ms. Rasch stated that there was concern by Mr. Moss and his Counsel as to how this all came about. After hearing argument, the Court appointed Mr. Gray as he was legally already appointed and the trust was not under court supervision at that time. Mr. Jay was re-appointed as Counsel on the Conservatorship matter.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.
